    55	
    56	    /// Returns the:
    57	    ///
    58	    /// - cursor bounds
    59	    /// - scroll offset
    60	    /// - current row index (No only the visible lines, but all lines)
    61	    ///
    62	    /// This method also will update for track scroll to cursor.
    63	    fn layout_cursor(
    64	        &self,
    65	        last_layout: &LastLayout,
    66	        bounds: &mut Bounds<Pixels>,
    67	        _: &mut Window,
    68	        cx: &mut App,
    69	    ) -> (Option<Bounds<Pixels>>, Point<Pixels>, Option<usize>) {
    70	        let state = self.state.read(cx);
    71	
    72	        let line_height = last_layout.line_height;
    73	        let visible_range = &last_layout.visible_range;
    74	        let lines = &last_layout.lines;
    75	        let text_wrapper = &state.text_wrapper;
    76	        let line_number_width = last_layout.line_number_width;
    77	
    78	        let mut selected_range = state.selected_range;
    79	
    80	        if let Some(ime_marked_range) = &state.ime_marked_range {
    81	            selected_range = (ime_marked_range.end..ime_marked_range.end).into();
    82	        }
    83	        let is_selected_all = selected_range.len() == state.text.len();
    84	
    85	        let mut cursor = state.cursor();
    86	        if state.masked {
    87	            // Because masked use `*`, 1 char with 1 byte.
    88	            selected_range.start = state.text.offset_to_char_index(selected_range.start);
    89	            selected_range.end = state.text.offset_to_char_index(selected_range.end);
    90	            cursor = state.text.offset_to_char_index(cursor);
    91	        }
    92	
    93	        let mut current_row = None;
    94	        let mut scroll_offset = state.scroll_handle.offset();
    95	        let mut cursor_bounds = None;
    96	
    97	        // If the input has a fixed height (Otherwise is auto-grow), we need to add a bottom margin to the input.
    98	        let top_bottom_margin = if state.mode.is_auto_grow() {
    99	            line_height
   100	        } else if visible_range.len() < BOTTOM_MARGIN_ROWS * 8 {
   101	            line_height
   102	        } else {
   103	            BOTTOM_MARGIN_ROWS * line_height
   104	        };
   105	
   106	        // The cursor corresponds to the current cursor position in the text no only the line.
   107	        let mut cursor_pos = None;
   108	        let mut cursor_start = None;
   109	        let mut cursor_end = None;
   110	
   111	        let mut prev_lines_offset = 0;
   112	        let mut offset_y = px(0.);
   113	        for (ix, wrap_line) in text_wrapper.lines.iter().enumerate() {
   114	            let row = ix;
   115	            let line_origin = point(px(0.), offset_y);
   116	
   117	            // break loop if all cursor positions are found
   118	            if cursor_pos.is_some() && cursor_start.is_some() && cursor_end.is_some() {
   119	                break;
   120	            }
   121	
   122	            let in_visible_range = ix >= visible_range.start;
   123	            if let Some(line) = in_visible_range
   124	                .then(|| lines.get(ix.saturating_sub(visible_range.start)))
   125	                .flatten()
   126	            {
   127	                // If in visible range lines
   128	                if cursor_pos.is_none() {
   129	                    let offset = cursor.saturating_sub(prev_lines_offset);
   130	                    if let Some(pos) = line.position_for_index(offset, last_layout) {
   131	                        current_row = Some(row);
   132	                        cursor_pos = Some(line_origin + pos);
   133	                    }
   134	                }
   135	                if cursor_start.is_none() {
   136	                    let offset = selected_range.start.saturating_sub(prev_lines_offset);
   137	                    if let Some(pos) = line.position_for_index(offset, last_layout) {
   138	                        cursor_start = Some(line_origin + pos);
   139	                    }
   140	                }
   540	            // Because masked use `*`, 1 char with 1 byte.
   541	            selected_range.start = state.text.offset_to_char_index(selected_range.start);
   542	            selected_range.end = state.text.offset_to_char_index(selected_range.end);
   543	        }
   544	
   545	        let (start_ix, end_ix) = if selected_range.start < selected_range.end {
   546	            (selected_range.start, selected_range.end)
   547	        } else {
   548	            (selected_range.end, selected_range.start)
   549	        };
   550	
   551	        let range = start_ix.max(last_layout.visible_range_offset.start)
   552	            ..end_ix.min(last_layout.visible_range_offset.end);
   553	
   554	        Self::layout_match_range(range, &last_layout, bounds)
   555	    }
   556	
   557	    /// Calculate the visible range of lines in the viewport.
   558	    ///
   559	    /// Returns
   560	    ///
   561	    /// - visible_range: The visible range is based on unwrapped lines (Zero based).
   562	    /// - visible_top: The top position of the first visible line in the scroll viewport.
   563	    fn calculate_visible_range(
   564	        &self,
   565	        state: &InputState,
   566	        line_height: Pixels,
   567	        input_height: Pixels,
   568	    ) -> (Range<usize>, Pixels) {
   569	        // Add extra rows to avoid showing empty space when scroll to bottom.
   570	        let extra_rows = 1;
   571	        let mut visible_top = px(0.);
   572	        if state.mode.is_single_line() {
   573	            return (0..1, visible_top);
   574	        }
   575	
   576	        let total_lines = state.text_wrapper.len();
   577	        let scroll_top = if let Some(deferred_scroll_offset) = state.deferred_scroll_offset {
   578	            deferred_scroll_offset.y
   579	        } else {
   580	            state.scroll_handle.offset().y
   581	        };
   582	
   583	        let mut visible_range = 0..total_lines;
   584	        let mut line_bottom = px(0.);
   585	        for (ix, line) in state.text_wrapper.lines.iter().enumerate() {
   586	            let wrapped_height = line.height(line_height);
   587	            line_bottom += wrapped_height;
   588	
   589	            if line_bottom < -scroll_top {
   590	                visible_top = line_bottom - wrapped_height;
   591	                visible_range.start = ix;
   592	            }
   593	
   594	            if line_bottom + scroll_top >= input_height {
   595	                visible_range.end = (ix + extra_rows).min(total_lines);
   596	                break;
   597	            }
   598	        }
   599	
   600	        (visible_range, visible_top)
   601	    }
   602	
   603	    /// Return (line_number_width, line_number_len)
   604	    fn layout_line_numbers(
   605	        state: &InputState,
   606	        text: &Rope,
   607	        font_size: Pixels,
   608	        style: &TextStyle,
   609	        window: &mut Window,
   610	    ) -> (Pixels, usize) {
   611	        let total_lines = text.lines_len();
   612	        let line_number_len = match total_lines {
   613	            0..=9999 => 5,
   614	            10000..=99999 => 6,
   615	            100000..=999999 => 7,
   616	            _ => 8,
   617	        };
   618	
   619	        let line_number_width = if state.mode.line_number() {
   620	            let empty_line_number = window.text_system().shape_line(
   621	                "+".repeat(line_number_len).into(),
   622	                font_size,
   623	                &[TextRun {
   624	                    len: line_number_len,
   625	                    font: style.font(),
   626	                    color: gpui::black(),
   627	                    background_color: None,
   628	                    underline: None,
   629	                    strikethrough: None,
   630	                }],
   631	                None,
   632	            );
   633	
   634	            empty_line_number.width + px(6.) + LINE_NUMBER_RIGHT_MARGIN
   635	        } else {
   636	            px(0.)
   637	        };
   638	
   639	        (line_number_width, line_number_len)
   640	    }
   641	
   642	    /// Compute inline completion ghost lines for rendering.
   643	    ///
   644	    /// Returns (first_line, ghost_lines) where:
   645	    /// - first_line: Shaped text for the first line (goes after cursor on same line)
   646	    /// - ghost_lines: Shaped lines for subsequent lines (shift content down)
   647	    fn layout_inline_completion(
   648	        state: &InputState,
   649	        visible_range: &Range<usize>,
   650	        font_size: Pixels,
   651	        window: &mut Window,
   652	        cx: &App,
   653	    ) -> (Option<ShapedLine>, Vec<ShapedLine>) {
   654	        if !state.should_show_inline_completion(window) {
   655	            return (None, vec![]);
   656	        }
   657	
   658	        let Some(completion_item) = state.inline_completion.item.as_ref() else {
   659	            return (None, vec![]);
   660	        };
   661	
   662	        // Get cursor row from cursor position
   663	        let cursor_row = state.cursor_position().line as usize;
   664	
   665	        // Only show if cursor row is visible
   666	        if cursor_row < visible_range.start || cursor_row >= visible_range.end {
   667	            return (None, vec![]);
   668	        }
   669	
   670	        let completion_text = &completion_item.insert_text;
   671	        let completion_color = cx.theme().muted_foreground.opacity(0.5);
   672	
   673	        let text_style = window.text_style();
   674	        let font = text_style.font();
   675	
   676	        let lines: Vec<&str> = completion_text.split('\n').collect();
   677	        if lines.is_empty() {
   678	            return (None, vec![]);
   679	        }
   680	
   681	        // Shape first line (goes after cursor)
   682	        let first_text: SharedString = lines[0].to_string().into();
   683	        let first_line = if !first_text.is_empty() {
   684	            let first_run = TextRun {
   685	                len: first_text.len(),
   686	                font: font.clone(),
   687	                color: completion_color,
   688	                background_color: None,
   689	                underline: None,
   690	                strikethrough: None,
   691	            };
   692	            Some(
   693	                window
   694	                    .text_system()
   695	                    .shape_line(first_text, font_size, &[first_run], None),
   696	            )
   697	        } else {
   698	            None
   699	        };
   700	
   701	        // Shape ghost lines (lines 2+ that shift content down)
   702	        let ghost_lines: Vec<ShapedLine> = lines[1..]
   703	            .iter()
   704	            .map(|line_text| {
   705	                let text: SharedString = line_text.to_string().into();
   706	                let len = text.len().max(1); // Ensure at least 1 for empty lines
   707	                let run = TextRun {
   708	                    len,
   709	                    font: font.clone(),
   710	                    color: completion_color,
   711	                    background_color: None,
   712	                    underline: None,
   713	                    strikethrough: None,
   714	                };
   715	                // Use space for empty lines so they take up height
   716	                let shaped_text = if text.is_empty() { " ".into() } else { text };
   717	                window
   718	                    .text_system()
   719	                    .shape_line(shaped_text, font_size, &[run], None)
   720	            })
   721	            .collect();
   722	
   723	        (first_line, ghost_lines)
   724	    }
   725	
   726	    fn layout_lines(
   727	        state: &InputState,
   728	        display_text: &Rope,
   729	        last_layout: &LastLayout,
   730	        font_size: Pixels,
   731	        runs: &[TextRun],
   732	        bg_segments: &[(Range<usize>, Hsla)],
   733	        window: &mut Window,
   734	    ) -> Vec<LineLayout> {
   735	        let is_single_line = state.mode.is_single_line();
   736	        let text_wrapper = &state.text_wrapper;
   737	        let visible_range = &last_layout.visible_range;
   738	        let visible_range_offset = &last_layout.visible_range_offset;
   739	
   740	        if is_single_line {
   741	            let shaped_line = window.text_system().shape_line(
   742	                display_text.to_string().into(),
   743	                font_size,
   744	                &runs,
   745	                None,
   746	            );
   747	
   748	            return vec![LineLayout::new().lines(smallvec::smallvec![shaped_line])];
   749	        }
   750	
   751	        // Empty to use placeholder, the placeholder is not in the text_wrapper map.
   752	        if state.text.len() == 0 {
   753	            return display_text
   754	                .to_string()
   755	                .split("\n")
   756	                .map(|line| {
   757	                    let shaped_line = window.text_system().shape_line(
   758	                        line.to_string().into(),
   759	                        font_size,
   760	                        &runs,

    45	struct ByteChunks<'a> {
    46	    cursor: ChunkCursor<'a>,
    47	    end: usize,
    48	}
    49	impl<'a> tree_sitter::TextProvider<&'a [u8]> for TextProvider<'a> {
    50	    type I = ByteChunks<'a>;
    51	
    52	    fn text(&mut self, node: tree_sitter::Node) -> Self::I {
    53	        let range = node.byte_range();
    54	        let cursor = self.0.chunk_cursor_at(range.start);
    55	
    56	        ByteChunks {
    57	            cursor,
    58	            end: range.end,
    59	        }
    60	    }
    61	}
    62	
    63	impl<'a> Iterator for ByteChunks<'a> {
    64	    type Item = &'a [u8];
    65	
    66	    fn next(&mut self) -> Option<Self::Item> {
    67	        let cursor = &mut self.cursor;
    68	        let end = self.end;
    69	
    70	        if cursor.next() && cursor.byte_offset() < end {
    71	            Some(cursor.chunk().as_bytes())
    72	        } else {
    73	            None
    74	        }
    75	    }
    76	}

   591	    pub fn styles(
   592	        &self,
   593	        range: &Range<usize>,
   594	        theme: &HighlightTheme,
   595	    ) -> Vec<(Range<usize>, HighlightStyle)> {
   596	        let mut styles = vec![];
   597	        let start_offset = range.start;
   598	
   599	        let highlights = self.match_styles(range.clone());
   600	
   601	        // let mut iter_count = 0;
   602	        for item in highlights {
   603	            // iter_count += 1;
   604	            let node_range = &item.range;
   605	            let name = &item.name;
   606	
   607	            // Avoid start larger than end
   608	            let mut node_range = node_range.start.max(range.start)..node_range.end.min(range.end);
   609	            if node_range.start > node_range.end {
   610	                node_range.end = node_range.start;
   611	            }
   612	
   613	            styles.push((node_range, theme.style(name.as_ref()).unwrap_or_default()));
   614	        }
   615	
   616	        // If the matched styles is empty, return a default range.
   617	        if styles.len() == 0 {
   618	            return vec![(start_offset..range.end, HighlightStyle::default())];
   619	        }
   620

   625	        //     println!("---- style: {:?} - {:?}", style.0, style.1.color);

   355	    timings,
   260	    noteElementSample: ids(activeNotesElements).slice(0, 80),
   261	  });
   262	
   263	  await driver.simulateKey("escape");
   264	  const dismissedNotesState = await waitFor(
   265	    "Notes spine dismissed",
   266	    () => notesState(driver!),
   267	    (state) => ((state.spine ?? {}) as Json).active !== true,
   268	    6000,
   269	  );
   270	  check("notes_escape_dismisses_spine", ((dismissedNotesState.spine ?? {}) as Json).active !== true, {
   271	    spine: dismissedNotesState.spine ?? null,
   272	  });
   273	
   274	  await setNotesText(driver, "@file:readme");
   275	  await sleep(250);
   276	  const contextNotesState = await notesState(driver);
   277	  const contextSpine = (contextNotesState.spine ?? {}) as Json;
   278	  check("notes_context_mentions_do_not_open_local_overlay", contextSpine.active !== true, {
   279	    spine: contextSpine,
   280	  });
   281	  check("notes_context_overlay_contract_false", contextSpine.contextMentionsLocalOverlay === false, {
   282	    spine: contextSpine,
   283	  });
   284	
   285	  await driver.close();
   286	  driverClosed = true;
   287	  check("driver_closed", true, {});
   288	} catch (error) {
   289	  check("probe_exception", false, {
   290	    message: error instanceof Error ? error.message : String(error),
   291	    stack: error instanceof Error ? error.stack : null,
   292	  });
   293	} finally {
   294	  if (driver && !driverClosed) {
   295	    await driver.close().catch(() => {});
   296	    driverClosed = true;
   297	  }
   298	}
   299	
   300	const receipt = {
   301	  schemaVersion: 1,
   302	  source: "runtime.dayNotesEditorHostContract",
   303	  pass: failures.length === 0,
   304	  failures,
   305	  binary,
   306	  sessionDir: driver?.sessionDir ?? null,
   307	  appLog: driver?.logPath ?? null,
   308	  checks,
   309	};
   310	
   311	await Bun.write(outPath, `${JSON.stringify(receipt, null, 2)}\n`);
   312	console.log(JSON.stringify(receipt, null, 2));
   313	process.exit(receipt.pass ? 0 : 1);

codex
The most plausible root cause is now clearer: `@title` comes from the block markdown grammar, but inline links in `[label](url)` live under markdown inline grammar. Since `markdown_inline` is registered but intentionally not injected, it is never run for those inline nodes during editable highlighting.

     9	/// An iterator over the lines of a `Rope`.
    10	pub struct RopeLines<'a> {
    11	    rope: &'a Rope,
    12	    row: usize,
    13	    end_row: usize,
    14	}
    15	
    16	impl<'a> RopeLines<'a> {
    17	    /// Create a new `RopeLines` iterator.
    18	    pub fn new(rope: &'a Rope) -> Self {
    19	        let end_row = rope.lines_len();
    20	        Self {
    21	            row: 0,
    22	            end_row,
    23	            rope,
    24	        }
    25	    }
    26	}
    27	impl<'a> Iterator for RopeLines<'a> {
    28	    type Item = RopeSlice<'a>;
    29	
    30	    #[inline]
    31	    fn next(&mut self) -> Option<Self::Item> {
    32	        if self.row >= self.end_row {
    33	            return None;
    34	        }
    35	
    36	        let line = self.rope.slice_line(self.row);
    37	        self.row += 1;
    38	        Some(line)
    39	    }
    40	
    41	    #[inline]
    42	    fn nth(&mut self, n: usize) -> Option<Self::Item> {
    43	        self.row = self.row.saturating_add(n);
    44	        self.next()
    45	    }
    46	
    47	    #[inline]
    48	    fn size_hint(&self) -> (usize, Option<usize>) {
    49	        let len = self.end_row - self.row;
    50	        (len, Some(len))
    51	    }
    52	}
    53	
    54	impl std::iter::ExactSizeIterator for RopeLines<'_> {}
    55	impl std::iter::FusedIterator for RopeLines<'_> {}
    56	
    57	/// An extension trait for [`Rope`] to provide additional utility methods.
    58	pub trait RopeExt {
    59	    /// Start offset of the line at the given row (0-based) index.
    60	    ///
    61	    /// # Example
    62	    ///
    63	    /// ```
    64	    /// use gpui_component::{Rope, RopeExt};
    65	    ///
    66	    /// let rope = Rope::from("Hello\nWorld\r\nThis is a test 中文\nRope");
    67	    /// assert_eq!(rope.line_start_offset(0), 0);
    68	    /// assert_eq!(rope.line_start_offset(1), 6);
    69	    /// ```
    70	    fn line_start_offset(&self, row: usize) -> usize;
    71	
    72	    /// Line the end offset (including `\n`) of the line at the given row (0-based) index.
    73	    ///
    74	    /// Return the end of the rope if the row is out of bounds.
    75	    ///
    76	    /// ```
    77	    /// use gpui_component::{Rope, RopeExt};
    78	    /// let rope = Rope::from("Hello\nWorld\r\nThis is a test 中文\nRope");
    79	    /// assert_eq!(rope.line_end_offset(0), 5); // "Hello\n"
    80	    /// assert_eq!(rope.line_end_offset(1), 12); // "World\r\n"
    81	    /// ```
    82	    fn line_end_offset(&self, row: usize) -> usize;
    83	
    84	    /// Return a line slice at the given row (0-based) index. including `\r` if present, but not `\n`.
    85	    ///
    86	    /// ```
    87	    /// use gpui_component::{Rope, RopeExt};
    88	    /// let rope = Rope::from("Hello\nWorld\r\nThis is a test 中文\nRope");
    89	    /// assert_eq!(rope.slice_line(0).to_string(), "Hello");
    90	    /// assert_eq!(rope.slice_line(1).to_string(), "World\r");
    91	    /// assert_eq!(rope.slice_line(2).to_string(), "This is a test 中文");
    92	    /// assert_eq!(rope.slice_line(6).to_string(), ""); // out of bounds
    93	    /// ```
    94	    fn slice_line(&self, row: usize) -> RopeSlice<'_>;
    95	
    96	    /// Return a slice of rows in the given range (0-based, end exclusive).
    97	    ///
    98	    /// If the range is out of bounds, it will be clamped to the valid range.
    99	    ///
   100	    /// ```
   101	    /// use gpui_component::{Rope, RopeExt};
   102	    /// let rope = Rope::from("Hello\nWorld\r\nThis is a test 中文\nRope");
   103	    /// assert_eq!(rope.slice_lines(0..2).to_string(), "Hello\nWorld\r");
   104	    /// assert_eq!(rope.slice_lines(1..3).to_string(), "World\r\nThis is a test 中文");
   105	    /// assert_eq!(rope.slice_lines(2..5).to_string(), "This is a test 中文\nRope");
   106	    /// assert_eq!(rope.slice_lines(3..10).to_string(), "Rope");
   107	    /// assert_eq!(rope.slice_lines(5..10).to_string(), ""); // out of bounds
   108	    /// ```
   109	    fn slice_lines(&self, rows_range: Range<usize>) -> RopeSlice<'_>;
   110	
   111	    /// Return an iterator over all lines in the rope.
   112	    ///
   113	    /// Each line slice includes `\r` if present, but not `\n`.
   114	    ///
   115	    /// ```
   116	    /// use gpui_component::{Rope, RopeExt};
   117	    /// let rope = Rope::from("Hello\nWorld\r\nThis is a test 中文\nRope");
   118	    /// let lines: Vec<_> = rope.iter_lines().map(|r| r.to_string()).collect();
   119	    /// assert_eq!(lines, vec!["Hello", "World\r", "This is a test 中文", "Rope"]);
   120	    /// ```
   121	    fn iter_lines(&self) -> RopeLines<'_>;
   122	
   123	    /// Return the number of lines in the rope.
   124	    ///
   125	    /// ```
   126	    /// use gpui_component::{Rope, RopeExt};
   127	    /// let rope = Rope::from("Hello\nWorld\r\nThis is a test 中文\nRope");
   128	    /// assert_eq!(rope.lines_len(), 4);
   129	    /// ```
   130	    fn lines_len(&self) -> usize;
   131	
   132	    /// Return the length of the row (0-based) in characters, including `\r` if present, but not `\n`.
   133	    ///
   134	    /// If the row is out of bounds, return 0.
   135	    ///
   136	    /// ```
   137	    /// use gpui_component::{Rope, RopeExt};
   138	    /// let rope = Rope::from("Hello\nWorld\r\nThis is a test 中文\nRope");
   139	    /// assert_eq!(rope.line_len(0), 5); // "Hello"
   140	    /// assert_eq!(rope.line_len(1), 6); // "World\r"
   141	    /// assert_eq!(rope.line_len(2), 21); // "This is a test 中文"
   142	    /// assert_eq!(rope.line_len(4), 0); // out of bounds
   143	    /// ```
   144	    fn line_len(&self, row: usize) -> usize;
   145	
   146	    /// Replace the text in the given byte range with new text.
   147	    ///
   148	    /// # Panics
   149	    ///
   150	    /// - If the range is not on char boundary.
   151	    /// - If the range is out of bounds.
   152	    ///
   153	    /// ```
   154	    /// use gpui_component::{Rope, RopeExt};
   155	    /// let mut rope = Rope::from("Hello\nWorld\r\nThis is a test 中文\nRope");
   156	    /// rope.replace(6..11, "Universe");
   157	    /// assert_eq!(rope.to_string(), "Hello\nUniverse\r\nThis is a test 中文\nRope");
   158	    /// ```
   159	    fn replace(&mut self, range: Range<usize>, new_text: &str);
   160	
   161	    /// Get char at the given offset (byte).
   162	    ///
   163	    /// - If the offset is in the middle of a multi-byte character will panic.
   164	    /// - If the offset is out of bounds, return None.
   165	    fn char_at(&self, offset: usize) -> Option<char>;
   166	
   167	    /// Get the byte offset from the given line, column [`Position`] (0-based).
   168	    ///
   169	    /// The column is in characters.
   170	    fn position_to_offset(&self, line_col: &Position) -> usize;
   171	
   172	    /// Get the line, column [`Position`] (0-based) from the given byte offset.
   173	    ///
   174	    /// The column is in characters.
   175	    fn offset_to_position(&self, offset: usize) -> Position;
   176	
   177	    /// Get point (row, column) from the given byte offset.
   178	    ///
   179	    /// The column is in bytes.
   180	    fn offset_to_point(&self, offset: usize) -> Point;
   181	
   182	    /// Get byte offset from the given point (row, column).
   183	    ///
   184	    /// The column is 0-based in bytes.
   185	    fn point_to_offset(&self, point: Point) -> usize;
   186	
   187	    /// Get the word byte range at the given byte offset (0-based).
   188	    fn word_range(&self, offset: usize) -> Option<Range<usize>>;
   189	
   190	    /// Get word at the given byte offset (0-based).
   191	    fn word_at(&self, offset: usize) -> String;
   192	
   193	    /// Convert offset in UTF-16 to byte offset (0-based).
   194	    ///
   195	    /// Runs in O(log N) time.
   196	    fn offset_utf16_to_offset(&self, offset_utf16: usize) -> usize;
   197	
   198	    /// Convert byte offset (0-based) to offset in UTF-16.
   199	    ///
   200	    /// Runs in O(log N) time.
   201	    fn offset_to_offset_utf16(&self, offset: usize) -> usize;
   202	
   203	    /// Get a clipped offset (avoid in a char boundary).
   204	    ///
   205	    /// - If Bias::Left and inside the char boundary, return the ix - 1;
   206	    /// - If Bias::Right and in inside char boundary, return the ix + 1;
   207	    /// - Otherwise return the ix.
   208	    ///
   209	    /// ```
   210	    /// use gpui_component::{Rope, RopeExt};
   211	    /// use sum_tree::Bias;
   212	    ///
   213	    /// let rope = Rope::from("Hello 中文🎉 test\nRope");
   214	    /// assert_eq!(rope.clip_offset(5, Bias::Left), 5);
   215	    /// // Inside multi-byte character '中' (3 bytes)
   216	    /// assert_eq!(rope.clip_offset(7, Bias::Left), 6);
   217	    /// assert_eq!(rope.clip_offset(7, Bias::Right), 9);
   218	    /// ```
   219	    fn clip_offset(&self, offset: usize, bias: Bias) -> usize;
   220	
   221	    /// Convert offset in characters to byte offset (0-based).
   222	    ///
   223	    /// Run in O(n) time.
   224	    ///
   225	    /// # Example
   226	    ///
   227	    /// ```
   228	    /// use gpui_component::{Rope, RopeExt};
   229	    /// let rope = Rope::from("a 中文🎉 test\nRope");
   230	    /// assert_eq!(rope.char_index_to_offset(0), 0);
   231	    /// assert_eq!(rope.char_index_to_offset(1), 1);
   232	    /// assert_eq!(rope.char_index_to_offset(3), "a 中".len());
   233	    /// assert_eq!(rope.char_index_to_offset(5), "a 中文🎉".len());
   234	    /// ```
   235	    fn char_index_to_offset(&self, char_index: usize) -> usize;
   236	
   237	    /// Convert byte offset (0-based) to offset in characters.
   238	    ///
   239	    /// Run in O(n) time.
   240	    ///
   241	    /// # Example
   242	    ///
   243	    /// ```
   244	    /// use gpui_component::{Rope, RopeExt};
   245	    /// let rope = Rope::from("a 中文🎉 test\nRope");
   246	    /// assert_eq!(rope.offset_to_char_index(0), 0);
   247	    /// assert_eq!(rope.offset_to_char_index(1), 1);
   248	    /// assert_eq!(rope.offset_to_char_index(3), 3);
   249	    /// assert_eq!(rope.offset_to_char_index(4), 3);
   250	    /// ```
   251	    fn offset_to_char_index(&self, offset: usize) -> usize;
   252	}
   253	
   254	impl RopeExt for Rope {
   255	    fn slice_line(&self, row: usize) -> RopeSlice<'_> {
   256	        let total_lines = self.lines_len();
   257	        if row >= total_lines {
   258	            return self.slice(0..0);
   259	        }
   260	
   261	        let line = self.line(row, LineType::LF);
   262	        if line.len() > 0 {
   263	            let line_end = line.len() - 1;
   264	            if line.is_char_boundary(line_end) && line.char(line_end) == '\n' {
   265	                return line.slice(..line_end);
   266	            }
   267	        }
   268	
   269	        line
   270	    }
   271	
   272	    fn slice_lines(&self, rows_range: Range<usize>) -> RopeSlice<'_> {
   273	        let start = self.line_start_offset(rows_range.start);
   274	        let end = self.line_end_offset(rows_range.end.saturating_sub(1));
   275	        self.slice(start..end)
   276	    }
   277	
   278	    fn iter_lines(&self) -> RopeLines<'_> {
   279	        RopeLines::new(&self)
   280	    }
   281	
   282	    fn line_len(&self, row: usize) -> usize {
   283	        self.slice_line(row).len()
   284	    }
   285	
   286	    fn line_start_offset(&self, row: usize) -> usize {
   287	        self.point_to_offset(Point::new(row, 0))
   288	    }
   289	
   290	    fn offset_to_point(&self, offset: usize) -> Point {
   291	        let offset = self.clip_offset(offset, Bias::Left);
   292	        let row = self.byte_to_line_idx(offset, LineType::LF);
   293	        let line_start = self.line_to_byte_idx(row, LineType::LF);
   294	        let column = offset.saturating_sub(line_start);
   295	        Point::new(row, column)
   296	    }
   297	
   298	    fn point_to_offset(&self, point: Point) -> usize {
   299	        if point.row >= self.lines_len() {
   300	            return self.len();
   301	        }
   302	
   303	        let line_start = self.line_to_byte_idx(point.row, LineType::LF);
   304	        line_start + point.column
   305	    }
   306	
   307	    fn position_to_offset(&self, pos: &Position) -> usize {
   308	        let line = self.slice_line(pos.line as usize);
   309	        self.line_start_offset(pos.line as usize)
   310	            + line
   311	                .chars()
   312	                .take(pos.character as usize)
   313	                .map(|c| c.len_utf8())
   314	                .sum::<usize>()
   315	    }
   316	
   317	    fn offset_to_position(&self, offset: usize) -> Position {
   318	        let point = self.offset_to_point(offset);
   319	        let line = self.slice_line(point.row);
   320	        let offset = line.utf16_to_byte_idx(line.byte_to_utf16_idx(point.column));
   321	        let character = line.slice(..offset).chars().count();
   322	        Position::new(point.row as u32, character as u32)
   323	    }
   324	
   325	    fn line_end_offset(&self, row: usize) -> usize {
   326	        if row > self.lines_len() {
   327	            return self.len();
   328	        }
   329	
   330	        self.line_start_offset(row) + self.line_len(row)
   331	    }
   332	
   333	    fn lines_len(&self) -> usize {
   334	        self.len_lines(LineType::LF)
   335	    }
   336	
   337	    fn char_at(&self, offset: usize) -> Option<char> {
   338	        if offset > self.len() {
   339	            return None;
   340	        }
   341	
   342	        self.get_char(offset).ok()
   343	    }
   344	
   345	    fn word_range(&self, offset: usize) -> Option<Range<usize>> {
   346	        if offset >= self.len() {
   347	            return None;
   348	        }
   349	
   350	        let mut left = String::new();
   351	        let offset = self.clip_offset(offset, Bias::Left);
   352	        for c in self.chars_at(offset).reversed() {
   353	            if c.is_alphanumeric() || c == '_' {
   354	                left.insert(0, c);
   355	            } else {
   356	                break;
   357	            }
   358	        }
   359	        let start = offset.saturating_sub(left.len());
   360	
   361	        let right = self
   362	            .chars_at(offset)
   363	            .take_while(|c| c.is_alphanumeric() || *c == '_')
   364	            .collect::<String>();
   365	
   366	        let end = offset + right.len();
   367	
   368	        if start == end { None } else { Some(start..end) }
   369	    }
   370	
   371	    fn word_at(&self, offset: usize) -> String {
   372	        if let Some(range) = self.word_range(offset) {
   373	            self.slice(range).to_string()
   374	        } else {
   375	            String::new()
   376	        }
   377	    }
   378	
   379	    #[inline]
   380	    fn offset_utf16_to_offset(&self, offset_utf16: usize) -> usize {
   381	        if offset_utf16 > self.len_utf16() {
   382	            return self.len();
   383	        }
   384	
   385	        self.utf16_to_byte_idx(offset_utf16)
   386	    }
   387	
   388	    #[inline]
   389	    fn offset_to_offset_utf16(&self, offset: usize) -> usize {
   390	        if offset > self.len() {
   391	            return self.len_utf16();
   392	        }
   393	
   394	        self.byte_to_utf16_idx(offset)
   395	    }
   396	
   397	    fn replace(&mut self, range: Range<usize>, new_text: &str) {
   398	        let range =
   399	            self.clip_offset(range.start, Bias::Left)..self.clip_offset(range.end, Bias::Right);
   400	        self.remove(range.clone());
   401	        self.insert(range.start, new_text);
   402	    }
   403	
   404	    fn clip_offset(&self, offset: usize, bias: Bias) -> usize {
   405	        if offset > self.len() {
   406	            return self.len();
   407	        }
   408	
   409	        if self.is_char_boundary(offset) {
   410	            return offset;
   411	        }
   412	
   413	        if bias == Bias::Left {
   414	            self.floor_char_boundary(offset)
   415	        } else {
   416	            self.ceil_char_boundary(offset)
   417	        }
   418	    }
   419	
   420	    fn char_index_to_offset(&self, char_offset: usize) -> usize {
   421	        self.chars().take(char_offset).map(|c| c.len_utf8()).sum()
   422	    }
   423	
   424	    fn offset_to_char_index(&self, offset: usize) -> usize {
   425	        let offset = self.clip_offset(offset, Bias::Right);
   426	        self.slice(..offset).chars().count()
   427	    }
   428	}
   429	
   430	#[cfg(test)]
   431	mod tests {
   432	    use ropey::Rope;
   433	    use sum_tree::Bias;
   434	    use tree_sitter::Point;
   435	
   436	    use crate::{RopeExt, input::Position};
   437	
   438	    #[test]
   439	    fn test_slice_line() {
   440	        let rope = Rope::from("Hello\nWorld\r\nThis is a test 中文\nRope");
   441	        assert_eq!(rope.slice_line(0).to_string(), "Hello");
   442	        assert_eq!(rope.slice_line(1).to_string(), "World\r");
   443	        assert_eq!(rope.slice_line(2).to_string(), "This is a test 中文");
   444	        assert_eq!(rope.slice_line(3).to_string(), "Rope");
   445	
   446	        // over bounds
   447	        assert_eq!(rope.slice_line(6).to_string(), "");
   448	
   449	        // only have \r end
   450	        let rope = Rope::from("Hello\r");
   451	        assert_eq!(rope.slice_line(0).to_string(), "Hello\r");
   452	        assert_eq!(rope.slice_line(1).to_string(), "");
   453	    }
   454	
   455	    #[test]
   456	    fn test_lines_len() {
   457	        let rope = Rope::from("Hello\nWorld\r\nThis is a test 中文\nRope");
   458	        assert_eq!(rope.lines_len(), 4);
   459	        let rope = Rope::from("");
   460	        assert_eq!(rope.lines_len(), 1);
   461	        let rope = Rope::from("Single line");
   462	        assert_eq!(rope.lines_len(), 1);
   463	
   464	        // only have \r end
   465	        let rope = Rope::from("Hello\r");
   466	        assert_eq!(rope.lines_len(), 1);
   467	    }
   468	
   469	    #[test]
   470	    fn test_lines() {
   471	        let rope = Rope::from("Hello\nWorld\r\nThis is a test 中文\nRope\r");
   472	        let lines: Vec<_> = rope.iter_lines().map(|r| r.to_string()).collect();
   473	        assert_eq!(
   474	            lines,
   475	            vec!["Hello", "World\r", "This is a test 中文", "Rope\r"]
   476	        );
   477	    }
   478	
   479	    #[test]
   480	    fn test_eq() {
   481	        let rope = Rope::from("Hello\nWorld\r\nThis is a test 中文\nRope");
   482	        assert!(rope.eq(&Rope::from("Hello\nWorld\r\nThis is a test 中文\nRope")));
   483	        assert!(!rope.eq(&Rope::from("Hello\nWorld")));
   484	
   485	        let rope1 = rope.clone();
   486	        assert!(rope.eq(&rope1));
   487	    }
   488	
   489	    #[test]
   490	    fn test_iter_lines() {
   491	        let rope = Rope::from("Hello\nWorld\r\nThis is a test 中文\nRope");
   492	        let lines: Vec<_> = rope
   493	            .iter_lines()
   494	            .skip(1)
   495	            .take(2)
   496	            .map(|r| r.to_string())
   497	            .collect();
   498	        assert_eq!(lines, vec!["World\r", "This is a test 中文"]);
   499	    }
   500	
   501	    #[test]
   502	    fn test_line_start_end_offset() {
   503	        let rope = Rope::from("Hello\nWorld\r\nThis is a test 中文\nRope");
   504	        assert_eq!(rope.line_start_offset(0), 0);
   505	        assert_eq!(rope.line_end_offset(0), 5);
   506	
   507	        assert_eq!(rope.line_start_offset(1), 6);
   508	        assert_eq!(rope.line_end_offset(1), 12);
   509	
   510	        assert_eq!(rope.line_start_offset(2), 13);
   511	        assert_eq!(rope.line_end_offset(2), 34);
   512	
   513	        assert_eq!(rope.line_start_offset(3), 35);
   514	        assert_eq!(rope.line_end_offset(3), 39);
   515	
   516	        assert_eq!(rope.line_start_offset(4), 39);
   517	        assert_eq!(rope.line_end_offset(4), 39);
   518	    }
   519	
   520	    #[test]
   521	    fn test_line_column() {
   522	        let rope = Rope::from("a 中文🎉 test\nRope");
   523	        assert_eq!(rope.position_to_offset(&Position::new(0, 3)), "a 中".len());
   524	        assert_eq!(
   525	            rope.position_to_offset(&Position::new(0, 5)),
   526	            "a 中文🎉".len()
   527	        );
   528	        assert_eq!(
   529	            rope.position_to_offset(&Position::new(1, 1)),
   530	            "a 中文🎉 test\nR".len()
   531	        );
   532	
   533	        assert_eq!(
   534	            rope.offset_to_position("a 中文🎉 test\nR".len()),
   535	            Position::new(1, 1)
   536	        );
   537	        assert_eq!(
   538	            rope.offset_to_position("a 中文🎉".len()),
   539	            Position::new(0, 5)
   540	        );
   541	    }
   542	
   543	    #[test]
   544	    fn test_offset_to_point() {
   545	        let rope = Rope::from("a 中文🎉 test\nRope");
   546	        assert_eq!(rope.offset_to_point(0), Point::new(0, 0));
   547	        assert_eq!(rope.offset_to_point(1), Point::new(0, 1));
   548	        assert_eq!(rope.offset_to_point("a 中".len()), Point::new(0, 5));
   549	        assert_eq!(rope.offset_to_point("a 中文🎉".len()), Point::new(0, 12));
   550	        assert_eq!(
   551	            rope.offset_to_point("a 中文🎉 test\nR".len()),
   552	            Point::new(1, 1)
   553	        );
   554	    }
   555	
   556	    #[test]
   557	    fn test_point_to_offset() {
   558	        let rope = Rope::from("a 中文🎉 test\nRope");
   559	        assert_eq!(rope.point_to_offset(Point::new(0, 0)), 0);
   560	        assert_eq!(rope.point_to_offset(Point::new(0, 1)), 1);
   561	        assert_eq!(rope.point_to_offset(Point::new(0, 5)), "a 中".len());
   562	        assert_eq!(rope.point_to_offset(Point::new(0, 12)), "a 中文🎉".len());
   563	        assert_eq!(
   564	            rope.point_to_offset(Point::new(1, 1)),
   565	            "a 中文🎉 test\nR".len()
   566	        );
   567	    }
   568	
   569	    #[test]
   570	    fn test_char_at() {
   571	        let rope = Rope::from("Hello\nWorld\r\nThis is a test 中文🎉\nRope");
   572	        assert_eq!(rope.char_at(0), Some('H'));
   573	        assert_eq!(rope.char_at(5), Some('\n'));
   574	        assert_eq!(rope.char_at(13), Some('T'));
   575	        assert_eq!(rope.char_at(28), Some('中'));
   576	        assert_eq!(rope.char_at(34), Some('🎉'));
   577	        assert_eq!(rope.char_at(38), Some('\n'));
   578	        assert_eq!(rope.char_at(50), None);
   579	    }
   580	
   581	    #[test]
   582	    fn test_word_at() {
   583	        let rope = Rope::from("Hello\nWorld\r\nThis is a test 中文 世界\nRope");
   584	        assert_eq!(rope.word_at(0), "Hello");
   585	        assert_eq!(rope.word_range(0), Some(0..5));
   586	        assert_eq!(rope.word_at(8), "World");
   587	        assert_eq!(rope.word_range(8), Some(6..11));
   588	        assert_eq!(rope.word_at(12), "");
   589	        assert_eq!(rope.word_range(12), None);
   590	        assert_eq!(rope.word_at(13), "This");
   591	        assert_eq!(rope.word_range(13), Some(13..17));
   592	        assert_eq!(rope.word_at(31), "中文");
   593	        assert_eq!(rope.word_range(31), Some(28..34));
   594	        assert_eq!(rope.word_at(38), "世界");
   595	        assert_eq!(rope.word_range(38), Some(35..41));
   596	        assert_eq!(rope.word_at(44), "Rope");
   597	        assert_eq!(rope.word_range(44), Some(42..46));
   598	        assert_eq!(rope.word_at(45), "Rope");
   599	    }
   600	
   601	    #[test]
   602	    fn test_offset_utf16_conversion() {
   603	        let rope = Rope::from("hello 中文🎉 test\nRope");
   604	        assert_eq!(rope.offset_to_offset_utf16("hello".len()), 5);
   605	        assert_eq!(rope.offset_to_offset_utf16("hello 中".len()), 7);
   606	        assert_eq!(rope.offset_to_offset_utf16("hello 中文".len()), 8);
   607	        assert_eq!(rope.offset_to_offset_utf16("hello 中文🎉".len()), 10);
   608	        assert_eq!(rope.offset_to_offset_utf16(100), 20);
   609	
   610	        assert_eq!(rope.offset_utf16_to_offset(5), "hello".len());
   611	        assert_eq!(rope.offset_utf16_to_offset(7), "hello 中".len());
   612	        assert_eq!(rope.offset_utf16_to_offset(8), "hello 中文".len());
   613	        assert_eq!(rope.offset_utf16_to_offset(10), "hello 中文🎉".len());
   614	        assert_eq!(rope.offset_utf16_to_offset(100), rope.len());
   615	    }
   616	
   617	    #[test]
   618	    fn test_replace() {
   619	        let mut rope = Rope::from("Hello\nWorld\r\nThis is a test 中文\nRope");
   620	        rope.replace(6..11, "Universe");
   621	        assert_eq!(
   622	            rope.to_string(),
   623	            "Hello\nUniverse\r\nThis is a test 中文\nRope"
   624	        );
   625	
   626	        rope.replace(0..5, "Hi");
   627	        assert_eq!(
   628	            rope.to_string(),
   629	            "Hi\nUniverse\r\nThis is a test 中文\nRope"
   630	        );
   631	
   632	        rope.replace(rope.len() - 4..rope.len(), "String");
   633	        assert_eq!(
   634	            rope.to_string(),
   635	            "Hi\nUniverse\r\nThis is a test 中文\nString"
   636	        );
   637	
   638	        // Test for not on a char boundary
   639	        let mut rope = Rope::from("中文");
   640	        rope.replace(0..1, "New");
   641	        // autocorrect-disable
   642	        assert_eq!(rope.to_string(), "New文");
   643	        let mut rope = Rope::from("中文");
   644	        rope.replace(0..2, "New");
   645	        assert_eq!(rope.to_string(), "New文");
   646	        let mut rope = Rope::from("中文");
   647	        rope.replace(0..3, "New");
   648	        assert_eq!(rope.to_string(), "New文");
   649	        // autocorrect-enable
   650	        let mut rope = Rope::from("中文");
   651	        rope.replace(1..4, "New");
   652	        assert_eq!(rope.to_string(), "New");
   653	    }
   654	
   655	    #[test]
   656	    fn test_clip_offset() {
   657	        let rope = Rope::from("Hello 中文🎉 test\nRope");
   658	        // Inside multi-byte character '中' (3 bytes)
   659	        assert_eq!(rope.clip_offset(5, Bias::Left), 5);
   660	        assert_eq!(rope.clip_offset(7, Bias::Left), 6);
   661	        assert_eq!(rope.clip_offset(7, Bias::Right), 9);
   662	        assert_eq!(rope.clip_offset(9, Bias::Left), 9);
   663	
   664	        // Inside multi-byte character '🎉' (4 bytes)
   665	        assert_eq!(rope.clip_offset(13, Bias::Left), 12);
   666	        assert_eq!(rope.clip_offset(13, Bias::Right), 16);
   667	        assert_eq!(rope.clip_offset(16, Bias::Left), 16);
   668	
   669	        // At character boundary
   670	        assert_eq!(rope.clip_offset(5, Bias::Left), 5);
   671	        assert_eq!(rope.clip_offset(5, Bias::Right), 5);
   672	
   673	        // Out of bounds
   674	        assert_eq!(rope.clip_offset(26, Bias::Left), 26);
   675	        assert_eq!(rope.clip_offset(100, Bias::Left), 26);
   676	    }
   677	
   678	    #[test]
   679	    fn test_char_index_to_offset() {
   680	        let rope = Rope::from("a 中文🎉 test\nRope");
   681	        assert_eq!(rope.char_index_to_offset(0), 0);
   682	        assert_eq!(rope.char_index_to_offset(1), 1);
   683	        assert_eq!(rope.char_index_to_offset(3), "a 中".len());
   684	        assert_eq!(rope.char_index_to_offset(5), "a 中文🎉".len());
   685	        assert_eq!(rope.char_index_to_offset(6), "a 中文🎉 ".len());
   686	
   687	        assert_eq!(rope.offset_to_char_index(0), 0);
   688	        assert_eq!(rope.offset_to_char_index(1), 1);
   689	        assert_eq!(rope.offset_to_char_index(3), 3);
   690	        assert_eq!(rope.offset_to_char_index(4), 3);
   691	        assert_eq!(rope.offset_to_char_index(5), 3);
   692	        assert_eq!(rope.offset_to_char_index(6), 4);
   693	        assert_eq!(rope.offset_to_char_index(10), 5);
   694	    }
   695	}

codex
I have enough to give the architect answer now: layout is effectively complete with a small regression-proofing gap; markdown links need a visible-range inline pass plus protocol/probe evidence.
codex
## Role Findings

P0 1 is functionally fixed by the current patch. The remaining work is to ratchet the geometry proof, not move more layout code.

Owners:
- `src/components/notes_editor/render.rs::NotesEditor::render_input`
- `src/components/notes_editor/render.rs::NotesEditor::render_input_state`
- `src/notes/window/render_editor_body.rs` call site using `self.notes_editor.read(cx).render_input(cx)`
- `src/notes/window/render_editor.rs` where the removed outer padding must stay removed

The runtime sample `NotesTitlebar y=0 h=36`, `NotesEditor y=36 h=216`, `NotesFooter y=252` proves the editor starts exactly below the titlebar. Add/keep a probe assertion that `editor.y >= titlebar.y + titlebar.h` and `footer.y >= editor.y + editor.h` for Notes, plus the existing shared render path checks.

P0 2 is not fixed yet. Day links are white because active markdown highlighting only proves the block markdown parser is running. `# heading` highlights because `src/notes/markdown_queries/markdown_highlights.scm` captures block grammar `(atx_heading (inline) @title)`. Inline links like `[Screenflow](scriptkit://...)` are parsed by `tree_sitter_md::INLINE_LANGUAGE`, and the current contract intentionally disables `markdown_inline` injection in `src/notes/markdown_highlighting.rs`. So `markdownInlineRegistered true` means the inline language exists, not that it is applied to editable text.

## Evidence And Assumptions

Evidence:
- `NotesEditor::new_markdown_pair` configures `InputState::code_editor("markdown")` in `src/components/notes_editor/component.rs`.
- `gpui-component` applies syntax in `vendor/gpui-component/crates/ui/src/input/element.rs::TextElement::highlight_lines` via `highlighter.styles(&range, &cx.theme().highlight_theme)`.
- The block query has `@title`, `@link_uri`, `@link_text`, but inline links require the inline grammar.
- `src/notes/markdown_queries/markdown_inline_highlights.scm` has the relevant captures for `(link_destination)`, `(uri_autolink)`, `(link_label)`, and `(link_text)`.
- `src/notes/markdown_highlighting.rs` explicitly keeps `markdown_inline` out of markdown injections for performance.

Assumption: the current screenshot text is in editable mode, not preview mode. Preview link styling is a separate path in `src/notes/markdown.rs` and is not the failing path.

## Failure Modes

- Re-enabling `markdown_inline` in `MARKDOWN_INJECTION_LANGUAGES` or `markdown_injections.scm` fixes colors but regresses scroll perf. Do not do this.
- Day-only styling fixes the screenshot but breaks the shared NotesEditor contract.
- Only asserting `markdownRegistered`, query fingerprints, or screenshot color is too weak. It misses exactly this bug: block highlighting active while inline captures are absent.
- A source-audit test for exact query text would be brittle. Prefer a behavior/highlighter test that proves ranges and styles.

## Recommendation

Fastest correct path:

1. Add a visible-range inline markdown overlay in the shared editable highlighting path.

Best owner:
- `vendor/gpui-component/crates/ui/src/highlighter/highlighter.rs::SyntaxHighlighter::styles`

Implementation shape:
- Keep the existing block markdown pass.
- If `self.language == "markdown"`, run a supplemental inline pass only for the requested `range`.
- Parse only that visible line/range with `markdown_inline` config from `LanguageRegistry`.
- Convert inline captures to the same `HighlightStyle` through `theme.style("link_text")` and `theme.style("link_uri")`.
- Combine with block styles using the existing highlight combination/merge behavior.
- Do not add `markdown_inline` to markdown injections.

If you want less vendor-specific surface area, add a small helper inside the same file, for example:
- `SyntaxHighlighter::markdown_inline_styles_for_visible_range`
- `SyntaxHighlighter::styles` calls it only for markdown

2. Normalize capture names in `src/notes/markdown_queries/markdown_inline_highlights.scm`.

Change:
- `@link_uri.markup` -> `@link_uri`
- `@link_text.markup` -> `@link_text`

Reason: vendored `SyntaxColors::style` maps exact names like `link_uri` and `link_text`; dotted suffixes only work if the resolver intentionally falls back by prefix. The block query already uses exact names.

3. Add semantic proof before screenshot proof.

Good test owners:
- `vendor/gpui-component/crates/ui/src/highlighter/highlighter.rs` tests, or a repo test that can instantiate `SyntaxHighlighter`
- `src/notes/markdown_highlighting.rs` tests for query compile plus runtime contract

Test assertion:
- Input: `# Heading\n[Screenflow](scriptkit://spine/file/screenflow)\n<https://eggo-brand.wzrrd.sh/>`
- Assert returned styles include non-default `color` or font style over exact byte ranges for:
  - `Screenflow` as `link_text`
  - `scriptkit://spine/file/screenflow` as `link_uri`
  - autolink URL as `link_uri`
- Assert `markdown_editor_runtime_info().inline_markdown_injection_disabled == true`.

4. Extend runtime probe evidence.

Owner:
- `scripts/agentic/day-notes-editor-runtime-parity-probe.ts`

Add a check that consumes new structured runtime metadata, not pixels. If needed, extend:
- `src/protocol/types/elements_actions_scriptlets.rs::ElementEditorRuntimeInfo`
- `src/components/notes_editor/component.rs::markdown_runtime_info_with_scroll`
- `src/notes/markdown_highlighting.rs`

Expose a compact `highlightSample` for the editor value/ranges, e.g. `{ role, text, range, styled }`, and assert Notes and Day both report styled `link_text` and `link_uri` while `inlineMarkdownInjectionDisabled` remains true.

Verification command set:
- `./scripts/agentic/agent-cargo.sh test markdown_highlighting`
- `./scripts/agentic/agent-cargo.sh test --lib notes`
- focused new highlighter test
- `SCRIPT_KIT_AGENT_ARTIFACT_NAME=day-notes-editor-fix ./scripts/agentic/agent-cargo.sh build --bin script-kit-gpui`
- `bun scripts/agentic/day-notes-editor-runtime-parity-probe.ts --binary target-agent/artifacts/day-notes-editor-fix/script-kit-gpui`

## Self Score

8/10. The layout conclusion is strong. The link root cause is strong because it explains the exact heading-vs-link split and preserves the perf constraint. The only uncertainty is the cleanest implementation seam inside the vendored highlighter, which should be resolved by a tiny focused test before editing.
